Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Xkcd thing, now interactive 1193 upvotes, 153 comments
  2. Physics Girl: Super-Kamiokande – Imaging the sun by detecting neutrinos [video] 442 upvotes, 70 comments
  3. The largest acidic geyser has been putting on quite a show 25 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. We've freed Cookie's Bustle from copyright hell 103 upvotes, 14 comments
  5. TorchLean: Formalizing Neural Networks in Lean 78 upvotes, 10 comments
  6. Time, Space, and Life as We Know It (2017) 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. An Interactive Intro to CRDTs (2023) 104 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 171 upvotes, 39 comments
  9. Voxile: A ray-traced game made in its own engine and programming language 131 upvotes, 31 comments
  10. Textadept 88 upvotes, 19 comments
  11. Graphics Programming Resources 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  12. Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents 74 upvotes, 19 comments
  13. Claude's Cycles [pdf] 525 upvotes, 225 comments
  14. Lenovo’s new ThinkPads score 10/10 for repairability 293 upvotes, 136 comments
  15. Weave – A language aware merge algorithm based on entities 28 upvotes, 9 comments
  16. I'm reluctant to verify my identity or age for any online services 902 upvotes, 553 comments
  17. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 19 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Don't become an engineering manager 321 upvotes, 235 comments
  19. GPT‑5.3 Instant 311 upvotes, 236 comments
  20. You can use newline characters in URLs 35 upvotes, 18 comments
  21. 130k Lines of Formal Topology: Simple and Cheap Autoformalization for Everyone? 16 upvotes, 6 comments
  22. Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 257 upvotes, 213 comments
  23. Mac external displays for designers and developers, part 2 14 upvotes, 6 comments
  24. MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max 705 upvotes, 702 comments
  25. When AI writes the software, who verifies it? 170 upvotes, 154 comments
  26. Mount Mayhem at Netflix: Scaling Containers on Modern CPUs 12 upvotes, 5 comments
  27. What’s in a name? (2014) 12 upvotes, 5 comments
  28. Vibe coding for PMs 32 upvotes, 23 comments
  29. Speculative Speculative Decoding (SSD) 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  30. California's Digital Age Assurance Act, and FOSS 3 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:00pm, 4 March 2026. Source here.
